role: A React toolbar region component that renders the top-level HUD for presence status, run controls, mode selection, graph editing actions, guided tours, settings, and notices.
capabilities: run-error surface with retry and dismiss
fit graph to viewport with padding
substance_why: It implements a complete, wired toolbar with context-driven state, multiple action buttons, a mode selector, error surface, guided-tour entries, and a mobile fold.
interactions_present: click tools-fold button to toggle mobile tool drawer
select mode from dropdown to change mode
click run button to trigger doRun
click retry button to retry doRun after error
click dismiss button to clear runError
interactions_missing: no keyboard shortcuts for toolbar actions
no ARIA labels/roles for screen readers
no disabled-state logic for selection-dependent actions
no loading/error feedback for non-run buttons
performance_risks: subscribes to large useApp context object without memoization, causing re-render on any app state change
inline arrow handlers recreated every render for every button
MODES map and dynamic title/className strings rebuilt each render
reuse_why: The toolbar's concepts and controls are central to the interface, but its monolithic layout and mobile-fold logic should be rebuilt in the redesign.
decomposition_hints: split mobile fold container from desktop toolbar layout
extract mode selector + description into its own component
